message:
chore: sync beads and fix evidence tests for new evidence_seqno field

Update test fixtures in evidence.rs to initialize the new evidence_seqno
field added to RuntimeDecision. The tests were failing because the struct
now requires this field.

Also sync beads issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -48,6 +48,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1m5.5","title":"CVE corpus normalization + deterministic scenario metadata","description":"Background:
- CVE validation must be reproducible and attributable, not a loose collection of ad-hoc PoCs.

Scope:
- Normalize CVE corpus metadata: vulnerability class, trigger preconditions, expected strict/hardened behavior, and evidence requirements.
- Build canonical scenario manifests for each CVE entry.

Deliverables:
1) CVE metadata schema.
2) Canonicalized corpus index.
3) Deterministic replay metadata per CVE.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Each CVE scenario has explicit expected outcomes in both modes.
- Corpus can be replayed deterministically.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for metadata schema validation.
- E2E corpus indexing and manifest replay tests.
- Logs: trace_id, cve_id, class, mode, expected_outcome, replay_key.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:28.444921159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:28.444921159Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cve","security","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1m5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1m5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:28.444921159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1m5.6","title":"Hardened CVE prevention/healing assertion suite","description":"Background:
- Hardened mode must provide concrete non-exploitability behavior for covered CVE classes.

Scope:
- Define hardened assertions per CVE (prevent, quarantine, safe-default, deny) and validate healing/repair actions.
- Ensure no crash/regression in covered exploit triggers.

Deliverables:
1) Hardened assertion matrix per CVE.
2) Healing action expectation map.
3) Regression checker for hardened CVE outcomes.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Covered CVE triggers do not lead to uncontrolled memory unsafety in hardened mode.
- Expected healing actions are emitted and logged.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for assertion evaluator.
- E2E hardened CVE trigger runs with artifact capture.
- Logs: trace_id, cve_id, healing_action, risk_state, outcome, artifact_refs.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:28.573960906Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:30.412801277Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cve","hardened","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1m5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1m5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:28.573960906Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1m5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1m5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:30.412724142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1m5.7","title":"Strict detection assertions + paired-mode CVE evidence runner","description":"Background:
- Strict mode should provide high-fidelity detection telemetry for unsafe patterns.

Scope:
- Define strict-mode detection assertions for each CVE scenario.
- Build unified CVE runner packaging strict+hardened evidence bundles.

Deliverables:
1) Strict detection expectation matrix.
2) Combined CVE runner with artifact dossier output.
3) CI gate for CVE regression.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Strict mode records expected unsafe detections for covered scenarios.
- Runner outputs complete and joinable evidence.

Testing/Logging:
- Unit tests for strict detection matching logic.
- E2E paired strict/hardened CVE runs.
- Logs: trace_id, cve_id, mode, detection_flags, verdict, dossier_ref.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:28.715965458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:32.219238061Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","cve","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1m5.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1m5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:28.715965458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1m5.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1m5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:30.572768705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1m5.7","depends_on_id":"bd-33p.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:05:32.219143755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-1o4k","title":"Verification matrix maintenance: backfill rows for all open critique beads + restore dashboard stats","description":"Background:
- scripts/check_verification_matrix.sh and harness integration tests enforce that every open/in_progress critique bead has a verification_matrix row and that dashboard stats are internally consistent.
- New critique beads have been added since bd-id3 closed, and verification_matrix.json dashboard regressed (missing by_obligation_type).

Scope:
- Ensure tests/conformance/verification_matrix.json contains entries for every open/in_progress critique bead in .beads/issues.jsonl.
- Restore dashboard.by_obligation_type and make dashboard totals/breakdowns consistent with entries.
- Keep changes deterministic and minimal-diff (stable ordering by bead_id).

Deliverables:
1) Updated tests/conformance/verification_matrix.json with missing rows backfilled.
2) Updated dashboard fields: total_critique_beads, total_entries, by_coverage_status, by_priority (with complete/partial/missing), by_obligation_type, by_stream.
3) Optional: add a tiny sync helper script if needed for determinism (but keep CI gates authoritative).

Acceptance:
- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness passes (including verification_matrix_test + matrix_drift_test).
- bash scripts/check_verification_matrix.sh passes.
- bash scripts/check_matrix_drift.sh passes (0 missing rows).

Notes:
- Do not delete stale rows for closed beads; closure gate relies on matrix rows for closed critique beads.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RusticCastle","created_at":"2026-02-12T21:38:53.805640595Z","created_by":"RusticCastle","updated_at":"2026-02-12T21:38:53.805640595Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","critique","verification"]}
 {"id":"bd-1om","title":"Perf: Golden snapshot outputs + sha256 regression gate","description":"Capture golden outputs for runtime_math telemetry so behavior drift is detectable.

Acceptance criteria:
- Produce one or more canonical snapshot JSONs under tests/ (or harness output fixtures).
- Store sha256 checksums; verify in CI/local gate.
- Golden set covers strict and hardened modes.

Rationale:
- We need isomorphism proofs for behavior changes; golden outputs are the cheap continuous check.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltForge","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:56.662031099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T05:46:29.172839801Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T05:46:29.172817930Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":17,"issue_id":"bd-1om","author":"CobaltForge","text":"Implemented golden runtime_math snapshot fixtures + sha256 regression gate.

- Added canonical golden snapshot fixture: `tests/runtime_math/golden/kernel_snapshot_smoke.v1.json` (covers strict + hardened via mode=both).
- Added checksum file: `tests/runtime_math/golden/sha256sums.txt` (format compatible with `sha256sum -c`).
- Added gate script: `scripts/snapshot_gate.sh`
  - Regenerates snapshot into `target/runtime_math_golden/` and validates against committed sha256sums.
  - On mismatch, prints a truncated diff vs the golden fixture.
- Added update script: `scripts/update_golden_snapshots.sh` to intentionally refresh the committed golden + sha256sums when behavior changes with an isomorphism proof.
- Wired into CI: `scripts/ci.sh` now runs `scripts/snapshot_gate.sh`.

Verification:
- `scripts/snapshot_gate.sh` passes locally (stable output / sha256 match).","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:46:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1oz","title":"EPIC: Fuzzing Infrastructure Completion","description":"Goal: Comprehensive fuzzing infrastructure to discover edge cases and vulnerabilities.

Background:
frankenlibc-fuzz crate exists but needs completion. Fuzzing should cover:
1. All ABI entrypoints with arbitrary C inputs
2. Membrane validation pipeline edge cases
3. Runtime math kernel state transitions
4. Allocator edge cases (size classes, alignment)

Fuzz Targets by Priority:
1. String operations (memcpy, strcpy, sprintf, etc.) - highest attack surface
2. Allocator (malloc/free/realloc sequences)
3. Format strings (printf family)
4. Resolver/network (DNS parsing, socket operations)
5. Locale/encoding (iconv, collation)

Infrastructure:
- cargo-fuzz integration (libFuzzer)
- OSS-Fuzz compatibility
- Corpus management and minimization
- Coverage-guided with structure-aware mutations
- CI integration with nightly fuzzing runs

Success Criteria:
- Zero crashes after 10B iterations per target
- All healing paths exercised and verified
- Coverage reaches 80%+ of membrane code
- No hangs or infinite loops discovered

Verification:
- Fuzz artifacts stored and reproducible
- Crash reports include TSM state snapshot
- Healing action distribution logged","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:58:03.059535087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:27.026824112Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzzing","security","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1oz","depends_on_id":"bd-1m5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:27.026782033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1oz","depends_on_id":"bd-2vv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:26.918923030Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1oz.1","title":"Fuzz target: string operations","description":"Fuzz string operations for edge cases.

Target Functions:
- memcpy, memmove, memset, memcmp, memchr
- strcpy, strncpy, strcat, strncat
- strlen, strcmp, strncmp
- strchr, strrchr, strstr
- sprintf, snprintf, sscanf

Fuzzing Strategy:
Structure-aware fuzzing with:
1. Variable source/dest buffer sizes
2. Overlapping regions
3. Null bytes in various positions
4. Maximum length strings
5. Misaligned pointers

Coverage Goals:
- All string function implementations in frankenlibc-core
- All membrane validation paths
- All healing action paths in hardened mode

Infrastructure:
- cargo-fuzz with libFuzzer
- Corpus seed with edge cases
- Nightly CI fuzzing (1M iterations)
- Crash reproduction scripts

Success Criteria:
- Zero crashes after 1B iterations
- All healing paths exercised
- Coverage report shows 80%+ of string code","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:16.212096377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:16.212096377Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzzing","string"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1oz.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1oz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:16.212096377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
